data.selected_code:
import pandas as pd
import os


def scrape_mvp_votes(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_votes = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        url = f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/awards/awards_{year}.html"
        tables = pd.read_html(url, header=1)
        try:
            mvp_table = tables[0]  # MVP table is the first table
            mvp_table["Year"] = year
            all_votes.append(mvp_table)
        except:
            print(f"Could not parse year {year}")

    df = pd.concat(all_votes)
    df = df.rename(columns={"Player": "Name", "Pts Won": "MVP_Points"})
    return df[["Year", "Name", "MVP_Points"]]


def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg


def scrape_multiple_seasons(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_stats = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        df = scrape_season_stats(year)
        all_stats.append(df)
    return pd.concat(all_stats)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if not os.path.exists("data"):
        os.makedirs("data")

    print("Scraping MVP votes...")
    mvp_df = scrape_mvp_votes()
    mvp_df.to_csv("data/mvp_votes.csv", index=False)

    print("Scraping player stats...")
    stats_df = scrape_multiple_seasons()
    stats_df.to_csv("data/season_stats.csv", index=False)

summary.low_structured:
• Scrapes MVP votes and player stats from Basketball Reference
• Saves the results as CSV files
summary.high_unstructured: This script defines functions to scrape NBA MVP voting results and player per-game statistics from Basketball Reference for a range of seasons (defaulting to 2018-2025). The scrape_mvp_votes function fetches and processes MVP tables, renames columns, and compiles the data into a DataFrame with year, player name, and MVP points. The scrape_season_stats function retrieves per-game stats for each season, filters out invalid or repeated rows, and keeps only key statistical columns. The main block ensures a 'data' directory exists, then saves the compiled MVP votes and player stats as separate CSV files, handling any missing or malformed data gracefully.
summary.medium_structured:
• Scrapes MVP vote data and player season stats from Basketball Reference
• Processes data for multiple years (default 2018-2025)
• Saves MVP votes and season stats to separate CSV files
  ◦ MVP votes: 'data/mvp_votes.csv'
  ◦ Player stats: 'data/season_stats.csv'
summary.medium_unstructured: The script collects NBA MVP voting data and player per-game statistics for multiple seasons from Basketball Reference. It processes and combines the data, then saves the results to CSV files in a local 'data' directory. The code handles missing data gracefully and ensures only relevant columns are kept.
summary.low_unstructured: This code scrapes MVP voting results and player season statistics from Basketball Reference and saves them as CSV files.
summary.high_structured:
• Defines functions to scrape NBA data from Basketball Reference:
  ◦ scrape_mvp_votes: Gathers MVP voting tables for each season, renames columns, and compiles results
  ◦ scrape_season_stats: Fetches per-game player stats, removes repeated headers, selects key columns (PTS, AST, TRB, FG%), and drops rows with missing values
  ◦ scrape_multiple_seasons: Aggregates season stats across multiple years
• Handles a range of years (default 2018-2025) for both MVP votes and stats
• Ensures the 'data' directory exists before saving files
• Saves processed data to CSV files:
  ◦ MVP votes to 'data/mvp_votes.csv'
  ◦ Player stats to 'data/season_stats.csv'
• Prints progress messages and handles errors in data scraping gracefully
summary.title: Basketball MVP Votes and Season Stats Scraper

import pandas as pd
import os


def scrape_mvp_votes(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_votes = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        url = f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/awards/awards_{year}.html"
        tables = pd.read_html(url, header=1)
        try:
            mvp_table = tables[0]  # MVP table is the first table
            mvp_table["Year"] = year
            all_votes.append(mvp_table)
        except:
            print(f"Could not parse year {year}")

    df = pd.concat(all_votes)
    df = df.rename(columns={"Player": "Name", "Pts Won": "MVP_Points"})
    return df[["Year", "Name", "MVP_Points"]]


def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg


def scrape_multiple_seasons(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_stats = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        df = scrape_season_stats(year)
        all_stats.append(df)
    return pd.concat(all_stats)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if not os.path.exists("data"):
        os.makedirs("data")

    print("Scraping MVP votes...")
    mvp_df = scrape_mvp_votes()
    mvp_df.to_csv("data/mvp_votes.csv", index=False)

    print("Scraping player stats...")
    stats_df = scrape_multiple_seasons()
    stats_df.to_csv("data/season_stats.csv", index=False)

        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns

data.updated_code:
def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    # Scrape per game stats
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid

    # Scrape advanced stats and merge
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year
    # Keep only Player, Year, and a few advanced stats (e.g., PER, WS, BPM if available)
    adv_cols = ["Player", "Year"]
    for col in ["PER", "WS", "BPM"]:
        if col in df_adv.columns:
            adv_cols.append(col)
    df_adv = df_adv[adv_cols]

    # Merge on Player and Year
    df_merged = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, how="left", left_on=["Player", "Year"], right_on=["Player", "Year"])

    return df_merged

def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg

data.updated_code:
def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year

    # Merge per-game and advanced stats on Player and Year
    merged_df = pd.merge(
        df_reg,
        df_adv,
        on=["Player", "Year"],
        suffixes=("_per_game", "_adv"),
        how="inner"
    )

    # Select relevant columns from both per-game and advanced stats
    columns_to_keep = [
        "Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%", "PER", "TS%"
    ]
    # Some advanced columns may not be present for all years, so filter existing ones
    columns_to_keep = [col for col in columns_to_keep if col in merged_df.columns]
    merged_df = merged_df[columns_to_keep]

    merged_df = merged_df.dropna(
        subset=[col for col in ["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"] if col in merged_df.columns]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return merged_df

  ◦ Adds a 'Year' column with the specified year to both datasets
• Merges per-game and advanced stats DataFrames on Player and Year
• Filters the merged DataFrame to include only:
  ◦ Player
  ◦ Year
  ◦ PTS (points per game)
  ◦ AST (assists per game)
  ◦ TRB (total rebounds per game)
  ◦ FG% (field goal percentage)

• Returns the cleaned and filtered DataFrame

data.new_code:
def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year

    # Merge per-game and advanced stats on Player and Year
    merged_df = pd.merge(
        df_reg,
        df_adv,
        on=["Player", "Year"],
        suffixes=("_per_game", "_adv"),
        how="inner"
    )

    # Select relevant columns from both per-game and advanced stats
    columns_to_keep = [
        "Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%", "PER", "TS%"
    ]
    # Some advanced columns may not be present for all years, so filter existing ones
    columns_to_keep = [col for col in columns_to_keep if col in merged_df.columns]
    merged_df = merged_df[columns_to_keep]

    merged_df = merged_df.dropna(
        subset=[col for col in ["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"] if col in merged_df.columns]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return merged_df

summaryData.high_unstructured: The scrape_season_stats function fetches both per-game and advanced NBA player statistics for a given year by constructing the appropriate Basketball Reference URLs and reading the first table from each page. It cleans both datasets by removing repeated header rows, adds a 'Year' column to each, and merges them on Player and Year. The function then filters the merged DataFrame to retain Player, Year, PTS, AST, TRB, FG%, and, where available, advanced stats such as PER and TS%. It drops any rows where these key statistics are missing to ensure data quality, and finally returns the cleaned DataFrame for further analysis or storage.

summaryData.low_unstructured: This function retrieves and processes both NBA per-game and advanced player statistics for a specified year.
summaryData.high_structured:
• Constructs URLs for both NBA per-game and advanced statistics for the specified year
• Reads the first table from each web page using pandas
• Cleans the data:
  ◦ Removes rows where the 'Player' column is equal to 'Player' (repeated headers) in both datasets
  ◦ Adds a 'Year' column with the specified year to both datasets
• Merges per-game and advanced stats DataFrames on Player and Year
• Filters the merged DataFrame to include only:
  ◦ Player
  ◦ Year
  ◦ PTS (points per game)
  ◦ AST (assists per game)
  ◦ TRB (total rebounds per game)
  ◦ FG% (field goal percentage)
  ◦ PER (player efficiency rating, if available)
  ◦ TS% (true shooting percentage, if available)
• Drops rows with missing values in any of the key statistics columns
• Returns the cleaned and filtered DataFrame

metadata.originalCode:
def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year

    # Merge per-game and advanced stats on Player and Year
    merged_df = pd.merge(
        df_reg,
        df_adv,
        on=["Player", "Year"],
        suffixes=("_per_game", "_adv"),
        how="inner"
    )

    # Select relevant columns from both per-game and advanced stats
    columns_to_keep = [
        "Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%", "PER", "TS%"
    ]
    # Some advanced columns may not be present for all years, so filter existing ones
    columns_to_keep = [col for col in columns_to_keep if col in merged_df.columns]
    merged_df = merged_df[columns_to_keep]

    merged_df = merged_df.dropna(
        subset=[col for col in ["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"] if col in merged_df.columns]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return merged_df

data.selected_code:
def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

summary.high_unstructured: The main() function orchestrates the end-to-end process of training and evaluating an XGBoost ranking model for MVP prediction. It first loads the dataset and splits it into training and testing sets based on years, using all years except the last two for training and the last two for testing. The function fits an XGBRanker model on the training data, predicts scores for the test set, and computes predicted ranks within each year. It then evaluates the model's ranking performance using the NDCG metric, prints detailed prediction results, and generates bar plots to visualize prediction errors for each test year.

metadata.originalCode:
def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

data.updated_code:
def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Try different n_estimators values and pick the best based on NDCG
    best_ndcg = -np.inf
    best_n_estimators = None
    best_model = None
    best_y_pred = None

    for n_estimators in [100, 1000, 2000]:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_estimators,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )

        print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_estimators}...")
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()} with n_estimators={n_estimators}: {ndcg:.4f}")

        if ndcg > best_ndcg:
            best_ndcg = ndcg
            best_n_estimators = n_estimators
            best_model = model
            best_y_pred = y_pred

    print(f"\nBest n_estimators: {best_n_estimators} with NDCG: {best_ndcg:.4f}")

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate using the best model
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = best_y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

90:     # Group sizes
91:     train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
92:     test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
93: 
94:     # Try different n_estimators values and pick the best based on NDCG
95:     best_ndcg = -np.inf
96:     best_n_estimators = None
97:     best_model = None
98:     best_y_pred = None
99: 
100:     for n_estimators in [100, 1000, 2000]:
101:         model = XGBRanker(
102:             objective="rank:pairwise",
103:             learning_rate=0.1,
104:             n_estimators=n_estimators,
105:             max_depth=4,
106:             random_state=42,
107:         )
108: 
109:         print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_estimators}...")
110:         model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)
111: 
112:         y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
113:         ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
114:         print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()} with n_estimators={n_estimators}: {ndcg:.4f}")
115: 
116:         if ndcg > best_ndcg:
117:             best_ndcg = ndcg
118:             best_n_estimators = n_estimators
119:             best_model = model
120:             best_y_pred = y_pred
121: 
122:     print(f"\nBest n_estimators: {best_n_estimators} with NDCG: {best_ndcg:.4f}")
123: 
124:     # Predict, sort, and evaluate using the best model
125:     df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = best_y_pred
126:     df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
127:         df.loc[test_idx]
128:         .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
129:         .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
130:     )
131: 
132:     # Show results
133:     print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
134:     plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
135:     plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

metadata.originalCode:
def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Try different n_estimators values and pick the best based on NDCG
    best_ndcg = -np.inf
    best_n_estimators = None
    best_model = None
    best_y_pred = None

    for n_estimators in [100, 1000, 2000]:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_estimators,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )

        print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_estimators}...")
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()} with n_estimators={n_estimators}: {ndcg:.4f}")

        if ndcg > best_ndcg:
            best_ndcg = ndcg
            best_n_estimators = n_estimators
            best_model = model
            best_y_pred = y_pred

    print(f"\nBest n_estimators: {best_n_estimators} with NDCG: {best_ndcg:.4f}")

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate using the best model
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = best_y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

Test set: the last two years
Prepares group sizes for ranking model training and evaluation
Trains multiple XGBRanker models with different n_estimators values and evaluates each using the NDCG metric
Selects the model with the highest NDCG score and uses it for prediction
Predicts scores for the test set and computes predicted ranks within each year using the best model
Outputs detailed prediction results including year, player name, true rank, predicted score, and predicted rank
Visualizes prediction errors for each test year by generating and saving bar plots

high_unstructured.summaryComponent: The main() function orchestrates the end-to-end process of training and evaluating an XGBoost ranking model for MVP prediction, now incorporating a grid search over n_estimators values.
It first loads the dataset
and splits it into training and testing sets based on years
using all years except the last two for training and the last two for testing.
The function fits multiple XGBRanker models with different n_estimators values on the training data
evaluates each using the NDCG metric on the test set
and selects the best-performing model.
It then predicts scores for the test set
computes predicted ranks within each year
prints detailed prediction results, and generates bar plots to visualize prediction errors for each test year.

def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

data.updated_code:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    # Grouped bar chart: group by Name, but if there are multiple entries per Name, show them side by side
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm", dodge=True)
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

data.new_code:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    # Grouped bar chart: group by Name, but if there are multiple entries per Name, show them side by side
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm", dodge=True)
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    # Grouped bar chart: group by Name, but if there are multiple entries per Name, show them side by side
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm", dodge=True)
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

metadata.originalCode:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

data.updated_code:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    # Create a grouped bar plot: for each player, display a single bar, but structure for grouped bars
    # Since there is only one value per player per year, we simulate grouping by using dodge=True
    sns.barplot(
        x="Name",
        y="RankError",
        hue="Name",
        data=year_df,
        palette="coolwarm",
        dodge=True
    )
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    # Create a grouped bar plot: for each player, display a single bar, but structure for grouped bars
    # Since there is only one value per player per year, we simulate grouping by using dodge=True
    sns.barplot(
        x="Name",
        y="RankError",
        hue="Name",
        data=year_df,
        palette="coolwarm",
        dodge=True
    )
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")

data.original_code:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    # Create a grouped bar plot: for each player, display a single bar, but structure for grouped bars
    # Since there is only one value per player per year, we simulate grouping by using dodge=True
    sns.barplot(
        x="Name",
        y="RankError",
        hue="Name",
        data=year_df,
        palette="coolwarm",
        dodge=True
    )
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

metadata.originalCode:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    # Create a grouped bar plot: for each player, display a single bar, but structure for grouped bars
    # Since there is only one value per player per year, we simulate grouping by using dodge=True
    sns.barplot(
        x="Name",
        y="RankError",
        hue="Name",
        data=year_df,
        palette="coolwarm",
        dodge=True
    )
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

metadata.originalCode:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    # Create a grouped bar plot: for each player, display a single bar, but structure for grouped bars
    # Since there is only one value per player per year, we simulate grouping by using dodge=True
    sns.barplot(
        x="Name",
        y="RankError",
        hue="Name",
        data=year_df,
        palette="coolwarm",
        dodge=True
    )
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

data.updated_code:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot true rank and predicted rank for a specific year, side by side for each player.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    # Prepare data for grouped bar plot: one bar for true rank, one for predicted rank per player
    plot_df = pd.melt(
        year_df,
        id_vars=["Name"],
        value_vars=["Rank", "PredictedRank"],
        var_name="RankType",
        value_name="RankValue"
    )
    # Map for better legend
    plot_df["RankType"] = plot_df["RankType"].map({"Rank": "True Rank", "PredictedRank": "Predicted Rank"})

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(
        x="Name",
        y="RankValue",
        hue="RankType",
        data=plot_df,
        palette="coolwarm",
        dodge=True
    )
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP True vs. Predicted Rankings ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

data.new_code:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot true rank and predicted rank for a specific year, side by side for each player.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    # Prepare data for grouped bar plot: one bar for true rank, one for predicted rank per player
    plot_df = pd.melt(
        year_df,
        id_vars=["Name"],
        value_vars=["Rank", "PredictedRank"],
        var_name="RankType",
        value_name="RankValue"
    )
    # Map for better legend
    plot_df["RankType"] = plot_df["RankType"].map({"Rank": "True Rank", "PredictedRank": "Predicted Rank"})

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(
        x="Name",
        y="RankValue",
        hue="RankType",
        data=plot_df,
        palette="coolwarm",
        dodge=True
    )
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP True vs. Predicted Rankings ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

metadata.originalCode:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot true rank and predicted rank for a specific year, side by side for each player.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    # Prepare data for grouped bar plot: one bar for true rank, one for predicted rank per player
    plot_df = pd.melt(
        year_df,
        id_vars=["Name"],
        value_vars=["Rank", "PredictedRank"],
        var_name="RankType",
        value_name="RankValue"
    )
    # Map for better legend
    plot_df["RankType"] = plot_df["RankType"].map({"Rank": "True Rank", "PredictedRank": "Predicted Rank"})

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(
        x="Name",
        y="RankValue",
        hue="RankType",
        data=plot_df,
        palette="coolwarm",
        dodge=True
    )
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP True vs. Predicted Rankings ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")
